questions.explanation: <main> semantically identifies the primary content of the document and should be unique per page.
questions.question: Which HTML5 element is most appropriate for wrapping the main content of a page (excluding header, footer, and sidebars)?

questions.explanation: Padding adds internal spacing between the content box and the border; margin is outside the border.
questions.question: In CSS3, which property is used to create space between an element's border and its content?

questions.explanation: aria-label supplies an accessible name that screen readers can announce when a visible label is missing.
questions.question: Which ARIA attribute is commonly used to provide an accessible name for an element when no visible label is present?

questions.explanation: Git is the standard distributed version control system used to track changes and collaborate on code.
questions.question: Which tool is primarily used for software configuration management (version control) in web development?

questions.explanation: Responsive design typically uses fluid layouts plus media queries to adapt the same page to different viewport sizes.

options.A: Use a fixed-width container (e.g., 960px) and center it
options.B: Use CSS media queries to change grid/flex rules at breakpoints
options.C: Serve a separate mobile site on m.example.com
options.D: Use images for text so it scales smoothly

questions.question: You want a layout to switch from a 1-column mobile view to a 3-column desktop view. Which approach best fits Responsive Web Design?

questions.explanation: The parameter is typed as string, so passing a number triggers a compile-time type error.

options.A: It compiles and runs, converting 42 to "42" automatically
options.B: It fails type-checking because 42 is not a string
options.C: It fails only at runtime because TypeScript types are erased
options.D: It works only if "noImplicitAny" is disabled

questions.explanation: Stable unique keys (like database ids) help React correctly match elements between renders and minimize DOM updates.

options.A: Use the array index as the key for every item
options.B: Omit the key prop to let React infer identity
options.C: Use a stable, unique key derived from the item (e.g., id)
options.D: Use Math.random() for the key to guarantee uniqueness

questions.question: In React, which pattern is recommended for rendering a list of items efficiently and avoiding reconciliation issues?

questions.explanation: srcset/sizes lets the browser choose an appropriately sized asset; modern formats reduce bytes while maintaining visual quality.

options.A: Replace all images with CSS gradients
options.B: Use responsive images with srcset/sizes and modern formats (e.g., AVIF/WebP)
options.C: Move all images into a single sprite sheet regardless of size
options.D: Inline all images as base64 in the HTML to reduce requests

questions.question: A page is slow on mobile due to large images. Which solution most directly improves performance while preserving quality across devices?

questions.explanation: A common incremental strategy is enabling allowJs/checkJs as needed and migrating file-by-file to .ts/.tsx to manage risk and effort.

options.A: Rename all .js files to .ts immediately and fix every error before running the app
options.B: Add TypeScript, allow JS files, and incrementally migrate by converting files to .tsx/.ts over time
options.C: Avoid a tsconfig.json and rely on editor hints only
options.D: Use TypeScript only for CSS modules and keep all logic in JavaScript

questions.question: You add TypeScript to an existing React project and want to type-check without rewriting everything. Which approach is most practical to start with?

questions.explanation: Console errors often immediately reveal exceptions (e.g., undefined variables) that stop event handlers or scripts from executing.

options.A: Open the Network panel and clear the cache
options.B: Open the Console panel and look for errors/exceptions when clicking the button
options.C: Open the Application panel and delete localStorage
options.D: Open the Performance panel and record a CPU profile without interacting

questions.question: A user reports that clicking a button does nothing. Using Browser Devtools, what is the most effective first step to identify whether a JavaScript error is preventing the click handler from running?
